patch.value: ## Discovery\n\nThe authenticated GitHub branch-protection endpoint reports master is not protected and the repository has no rulesets. This permits force pushes/deletion and does not require the established CI/security checks.
Protect the default branch with required quality checks
Enable GitHub branch protection so unverified commits cannot silently become the default branch.

master blocks force pushes and deletion; current CI/security status checks are required with strict up-to-date branches; owner retains an explicit emergency admin bypass; settings are verified through gh after merge

value.author: codex-root
value.text: gh api repos/unbraind/homeassistant-cli/branches/master/protection returned Branch not protected; repository rulesets list is empty.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Apply protection only after the remediation PR merges so new required checks are known-good and do not block this bootstrap delivery.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Security workflows do not protect the default branch unless repository policy requires their successful contexts.

author: codex-root
message: Begin branch protection remediation; application waits for merged checks

author: codex-root
message: Begin branch protection remediation; application waits for merged checks

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Applied master protection after merge. GitHub confirms strict up-to-date required contexts for Bun/Node quality, coverage, package smoke, commit audit, history scan, CodeQL, dependency audit, and PR title; PR workflow, linear history, stale-review dismissal, conversation resolution, and force-push/deletion blocks are enabled. Admin enforcement remains disabled as the explicit emergency owner bypass.

patch.value: Protected master with PR-only changes, nine required current checks, linear history, conversation resolution, and destructive-update blocks.
Unverified or destructive non-admin changes cannot enter master; the owner retains an emergency bypass.
GitHub branch-protection API returned the requested policy with strict checks and all protective flags enabled.
Default-branch protection is active and verified through the GitHub API.
